UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user within the EvenTide application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: User's email address for login and communication.

role.description: User's role within the application (Owner, Planner, Guest, Security, Cohost, Hotelier, Hall Owner, Car Hire Service).

phoneNumber.description: User's phone number for contact purposes.

Event.title: Event
Event.description: Represents an event created within the EvenTide application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Event entity.

ownerId.description: Reference to UserProfile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Event) The user ID of the event owner.

plannerId.description: Reference to UserProfile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Event) The user ID of the event planner (can be null if no planner is assigned).

eventCode.description: Unique event code generated by the system (e.g., WEO-O2CAP5).

eventType.description: Type of event (Wedding, General Event, Birthday, etc.).  Determines the prefix of the event code.

eventDate.description: Date and time of the event.

primaryColor.description: Primary color chosen for the event's theme.

secondaryColor.description: Secondary color chosen for the event's theme.

isPublic.description: Indicates whether the event is public or private.  Determines visibility of media.

Guest.title: Guest
Guest.description: Represents a guest invited to an event.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Guest entity.

eventId.description: Reference to Event. (Relationship: Event 1:N Guest) The event this guest is invited to.

userProfileId.description: Reference to UserProfile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Guest)  The user account of this guest, if they have one.

guestCode.description: Unique guest code for gatepass validation.

rsvpStatus.description: RSVP status of the guest (Accepted, Declined, Maybe).

checkedIn.description: Indicates whether the guest has checked in to the event.

Announcement.title: Announcement
Announcement.description: Represents a broadcast announcement for a specific event.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Announcement entity.

eventId.description: Reference to Event. (Relationship: Event 1:N Announcement) The event to which this announcement belongs.

authorId.description: Reference to UserProfile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Announcement) The user ID of the author of the announcement.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the announcement was created.

Chat.title: Chat
Chat.description: Represents a chat message associated with an event.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Chat message entity.

eventId.description: Reference to Event. (Relationship: Event 1:N Chat) The event to which this chat message belongs.

senderId.description: Reference to UserProfile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Chat) The user ID of the sender of the chat message.

recipientId.description: Reference to UserProfile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Chat) The user ID of the recipient of the chat message (can be null for general event chat).

message.description: The content of the chat message.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the chat message was sent.

Media.title: Media
Media.description: Represents media (photos or videos) uploaded by guests at an event.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Media entity.

eventId.description: Reference to Event. (Relationship: Event 1:N Media) The event to which this media belongs.

uploaderId.description: Reference to UserProfile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Media) The user ID of the user who uploaded the media.

fileUrl.description: URL of the uploaded media file.

uploadTimestamp.description: Timestamp of when the media was uploaded.

PricePlan.title: PricePlan
PricePlan.description: Represents the different pricing plans available for event creation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PricePlan entity.

name.description: Name of the price plan (Free, Standard, Gold, Platinum).

maxGuests.description: Maximum number of guests allowed for this plan.

securityPersonnel.description: Number of security personnel allowed for this plan.

planners.description: Number of planners allowed for this plan.

cohosts.description: Number of cohosts allowed for this plan.

vipGuests.description: Number of VIP guests allowed for this plan.

price.description: Cost of the price plan in Naira.

EventCohost.title: EventCohost
EventCohost.description: Represents the association between an event and a cohost.

id.description: Unique identifier for the EventCohost entity.

eventId.description: Reference to Event. (Relationship: Event N:N UserProfile) The event the cohost is associated with.

userProfileId.description: Reference to UserProfile. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Event) The user ID of the cohost.

Testimonial.title: Testimonial
Testimonial.description: A user-submitted testimonial about their experience with the EvenTide platform.

name.description: The name or business name of the person submitting the testimonial.

role.description: The role of the person on the platform (e.g., Event Owner / Host, Planner, Vendor, Guest).

isApproved.description: Moderation status. True if the testimonial is approved to be shown publicly.

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the testimonial was submitted.

AdRequest.title: AdRequest
AdRequest.description: Represents an advertising proposal submitted by a user or business.

name.description: The name or business name of the person submitting the proposal.

email.description: The contact email for the advertiser.

subject.description: A concise subject line for the advertising campaign.

concept.description: A detailed description of the advertisement concept, target audience, and desired placement.

status.description: The current moderation status of the request (e.g., pending, approved, rejected).

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the request was submitted.

Hotel.title: Hotel
Hotel.description: Represents a hotel listing on the EvenTide platform.

ownerId.description: The UID of the hotelier who owns this listing.

state.description: The Nigerian state where the hotel is located.

city.description: The city where the hotel is located.

address.description: The full street address of the hotel.

description.description: A detailed summary of the hotel's features and amenities.

imageUrls.description: An array of URLs for the hotel's images.

amenities.description: A list of amenities offered by the hotel (e.g., Wi-Fi, Pool, Gym).

roomTypes.description: A list of available room types with their details.

Booking.title: Booking
Booking.description: Represents a booking request for a hotel room.

Venue.title: Venue
Venue.description: Represents an event venue listing on the EvenTide platform.

ownerId.description: The UID of the Hall Owner who owns this listing.

state.description: The Nigerian state where the venue is located.

city.description: The city where the venue is located.

address.description: The full street address of the venue.

description.description: A summary of the venue's features and amenities.

imageUrls.description: An array of URLs for the venue's images.

capacity.description: The maximum guest capacity of the venue.

amenities.description: A list of amenities offered by the venue (e.g., Parking, Wi-Fi, Stage).

features.description: A list of special features of the venue (e.g., Projector, Sound System).

VenueBooking.title: VenueBooking
VenueBooking.description: Represents a booking request for a venue.

SupportTicket.title: SupportTicket
SupportTicket.description: Represents a support ticket submitted by a user.

name.description: The name of the user submitting the ticket.

email.description: The contact email for the user.

subject.description: The subject of the support ticket.

message.description: The detailed message of the support ticket.

status.description: The current status of the support ticket (e.g., open, in-progress, closed).

priority.description: The priority of the support ticket (e.g., low, medium, high).

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the support ticket was submitted.

Car.title: Car
Car.description: Represents a car listing for hire on the EvenTide platform.

ownerId.description: The UID of the Car Hire Service provider who owns this listing.

make.description: The manufacturer of the car (e.g., Toyota, Mercedes-Benz).

model.description: The model of the car (e.g., Camry, C-Class).

year.description: The manufacturing year of the car.

pricePerDay.description: The rental price for the car per day in Naira.

imageUrls.description: An array of URLs for the car's images.

features.description: A list of features for the car (e.g., AC, Automatic, Sunroof).

CarBooking.title: CarBooking
CarBooking.description: Represents a booking request for a car.

definition.description: Stores user profile data. Includes 'role' for authorization. Path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores event details. Includes 'ownerId' and denormalized event-level authorization data. Segregation for QAPs.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to ensure security, scalability, and ease of debugging, following the principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC, and QAPs. Authorization independence is achieved through denormalization. For example, the `Event` document will have denormalized fields from `UserProfile` such as owner's role.  

**Explanation of the Structure and How it Meets the Core Design Principles:**

1.  **Authorization Independence:**
    *   Avoids `get()` calls in security rules by denormalizing relevant authorization data. For example, the `Event` document stores `ownerId`, which allows security rules to directly check the user ID against the event's owner without needing to fetch the owner's profile. The `guests` subcollection will store the eventID to easily manage listing.

2.  **Clarity of Intent:**
    *   The structure uses descriptive path names and field names to clearly indicate the purpose of each collection and document. For example, `/users/{userId}/events/{eventId}` clearly indicates that events are owned by users.

3.  **DBAC (Database-Based Access Control):**
    *   User roles are stored directly in the `UserProfile` documents (the 'role' property), eliminating the need for custom claims. Security rules can directly check the user's role in their profile.

4.  **QAPs (Rules Are Not Filters):**
    *   The structure supports secure list operations. Path-based ownership (e.g., `/users/{userId}/events`) allows listing only the events owned by a specific user. Segregation also helps; for instance, private data is stored under the user's path, while potentially public data (if any) would be in a separate collection with appropriate rules.

5.  **Invariants:**
    *   The structure supports the integrity of ownership, timestamps, and denormalized data through security rules that enforce constraints on data modification. For instance, the `ownerId` field in the `Event` document can only be set during creation and cannot be changed afterward.

**Denormalization Strategy:**

*   Authorization data is denormalized to avoid `get()` calls in security rules. For instance:
    *   `Event` documents include the `ownerId` from `UserProfile`.
    *   Subcollections requiring parent authorization data will include copies of the relevant fields.

**Structural Segregation:**

*   Data with different access needs are stored in separate collections:
    *   User profiles are stored in `/users/{userId}` for private user data.
    *   Events are stored in `/events/{eventId}` with access control based on the owner.

**Access Modeling:**

*   Uses path-based ownership for private data: `/users/{userId}/events/{eventId}`.
*   Employs the membership map pattern for collaborative data (if needed in the future).

**Data Clarity and Predictability:**

*   Explicit state modeling: Using a `status` field for events (e.g., `DRAFT`, `PUBLISHED`).
*   Predictable schema: Avoiding dynamic keys and using arrays of objects with static keys instead.
*   Radical consistency: Enforcing strict naming conventions and standardizing authorization fields (e.g., always `ownerId`).
